heroHeadline: Oil Jumps as Iran-US Talks Stall, Hormuz Constrained — 'Super Week' Opens April 28: FOMC Hold, Warsh Senate Vote April 29, Q1 GDP & March PCE April 30
heroSubtitle: Apr 26: J.P. Morgan Global Research shifted its base case to NO Fed rate cuts in 2026 (possible hike Q3 2027) — the most hawkish major-bank revision since the Iran war began — as stagflation (CPI 3.3%, Michigan ATL 47.6, Brent ~$105) makes 2026 easing functionally impossible. The Senate Banking Committee confirmed Kevin Warsh's nomination vote for Wednesday April 29 at 10:00 AM ET, simultaneously with the FOMC's second meeting day (decision 2 PM, Powell's final press conference 2:30 PM). The S&P 500 settled near its all-time high of ~7,165 (Nasdaq ~24,836) entering the most consequential macro week of 2026: BOJ decision April 28 (expected hold at 0.75%), FOMC hold at 3.50-3.75% (99.7% probability), Conference Board Consumer Confidence April 29, Q1 2026 GDP advance estimate April 30 (GDPNow 1.24% vs NY Fed 2.09%), March PCE inflation April 30 (expected ~3.4% YoY), ECB hold April 29-30, and mega-cap tech earnings (MSFT, GOOGL, META April 29; AAPL April 30). Recession probabilities: Goldman Sachs 30%, JPMorgan 35%, Moody's Analytics 49%.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, US Census Bureau, S&P Global Flash PMI Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, The National News, Al Jazeera, NPR, NBC News, 24/7 Wall St, OneIndia, Fortune, Motley Fool, Washington Post, Senate Banking Committee, Japan Times, Polymarket, TradingKey.
